settings.title: CKEditor5 Settings
settings.notice: CKEditor5 is loaded via CDN. An internet connection is required. It is free to use without a separate license.
settings.section_preview_hint: Preview CKEditor5 with the current settings applied.
settings.preview_placeholder: Type here to test the editor.

toolbar.label: Toolbar Type
toolbar.hint: Select the toolbar button configuration for the editor.
toolbar.standard: Standard (Heading, Format, Alignment, Link, List, Table)
toolbar.full: Full (Standard + Media, Colors, Code Block, Source Editing)

image_upload.label: Enable Image Upload
image_upload.hint: Enable direct image upload in the editor. When disabled, only URL input is available.

image_max_size.label: Max Image Size (MB)
image_max_size.hint: Maximum allowed size for uploaded images. (1 ~ 10 MB)

editor_height.label: Editor Height (px)
editor_height.hint: Set the minimum height of the editor in pixels. (200 ~ 2000 px)

guide.title: Usage Guide
guide.step1: Once activated, CKEditor5 is automatically applied to areas using extension_point html_editor and html_content in layouts. Areas using HtmlEditor/HtmlContent components directly (e.g., mail templates) are not affected.
guide.step2: Select the toolbar type to adjust the features displayed in the editor. Choose a toolbar that fits your use case.
guide.step3: Enabling image upload allows users to paste or upload images directly in the editor. Uploaded images are stored on the server.
guide.step4: Changes take effect immediately after saving. No page refresh is required.
guide.step5: When deactivated, editors revert to the default HtmlEditor and content display reverts to the default HtmlContent.
